blocks.content:
By default, a data source variable lets users select one instance at a time. Enabling multi-value turns the dropdown into a multi-select, so users can query several instances simultaneously. Grafana creates a separate query for each selected instance and combines the results in the panel.

To enable multi-value selection, complete the following steps:

Click the **Dashboard settings** gear icon in the toolbar.

If you're not in edit mode, click **Edit** first.
In the settings sidebar, click **Variables**, then click the variable you created to open its editor.

blocks.reftarget: data-testid Variable editor Form Multi switch

blocks.content: Click **Back to list** to save the change.
Return to the dashboard. The variable dropdown now supports multi-select. Select two or more instances and observe the panel update with data from all selected sources.

With multi-value enabled, a single dashboard can serve teams that need to compare data across regions, environments, or clusters — all without duplicating queries or panels.

In the next milestone, you wrap up and review what you've built.
